name: Education
description: EdTech AI regulation covering algorithmic assessment, adaptive learning, student profiling, and academic integrity systems.

highRiskCategories.category: AI systems determining access to education
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for the purpose of determining access or admission to educational and vocational training institutions at all levels.
AI systems intended to be used for the purpose of evaluating learning outcomes, including those used to steer the learning process.

highRiskCategories.category: AI for assessing appropriate education level
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for the purpose of assessing the appropriate level of education that an individual will receive or be able to access.

highRiskCategories.category: AI monitoring student behavior during exams
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for monitoring and detecting prohibited behaviour of students during tests in the context of or within educational institutions.

sectorRequirements.requirement: AI systems used for student admissions, grading, or determining educational access must undergo conformity assessment as high-risk AI systems, with documented quality management and risk management systems.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 6, Annex III para 3; ISO 42001 Clause 6; OECD AI Principles

sectorRequirements.requirement: Implement enhanced data protection measures for student data used in AI systems, including age-appropriate privacy safeguards, parental consent mechanisms, and restrictions on behavioral profiling of minors.
sectorRequirements.source: GDPR Art. 8 (children's consent); EU AI Act Recital 47; UNESCO AI Ethics; Malaysia PDPA 2010; COPPA (US)

sectorRequirements.requirement: Ensure algorithmic assessment systems provide transparent scoring criteria and enable students and educators to understand the factors contributing to evaluations and recommendations.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 13, Art. 86; UNESCO AI Ethics Transparency; Malaysia NGAIGE Transparency Principle

sectorRequirements.requirement: Conduct fairness assessments of AI-driven educational tools to ensure they do not discriminate based on socioeconomic background, disability, language, ethnicity, or gender.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 10; UNESCO AI Ethics Fairness; OECD AI Inclusive Growth; Malaysia NGAIGE Fairness Principle

sectorRequirements.requirement: Maintain human oversight of AI proctoring and exam monitoring systems, with clear appeal mechanisms for students flagged for suspected misconduct by automated systems.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 14, Annex III para 3(d); UNESCO AI Ethics; UK AI Framework Contestability

sectorRequirements.requirement: Prohibit emotion recognition AI in educational settings except where medically necessary, and restrict continuous behavioral monitoring of students to proportionate and justified use cases.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 5(1)(f); UNESCO AI Ethics; OECD AI Principles Safety

sectorRequirements.requirement: Assess the societal impact of adaptive learning AI on educational equity, ensuring that personalized learning systems do not create or widen achievement gaps between student groups.
sectorRequirements.source: UNESCO AI Ethics; OECD AI Inclusive Growth; Malaysia NGAIGE Inclusiveness; EU AI Act Recital 47

keyRisks.description: AI systems used in admissions may encode historical biases from institutional selection patterns, systematically disadvantaging applicants from underrepresented backgrounds.
AI proctoring and behavioral monitoring tools may create chilling effects on learning environments and disproportionately impact students with disabilities or non-standard testing needs.
AI-driven personalized learning may create self-reinforcing loops that limit student exposure to challenging material based on early performance indicators.
AI plagiarism and integrity detection tools may produce false accusations with severe academic consequences, disproportionately affecting non-native language users.
